summary: Tested StudyOS v3 features: /api/me/limits, study-plan Free gate (402), support chat (POST + history), material chat 10/day gate guard, plus full frontend pass for new logo, onboarding modal, support chat drawer, Pricing copy updates, dashboard limits card, and material chat entry. 9/9 backend pytest passed. Frontend mostly green with ONE blocking UI bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Floating LifeBuoy button at bottom-6 right-6 is covered by the 'Made with Emergent' badge anchor (id=emergent-badge) which intercepts pointer events. Playwright click was blocked even with force=True; user cannot reliably open the support drawer on any page where the badge is rendered. Re-position the button (e.g. bottom-20 right-6 or bottom-6 right-24) or raise it above the badge with a higher z-index AND offset so it is not occluded.

issues: Console accessibility warnings: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle' and missing aria-describedby. Wrap the heading in shadcn DialogTitle (or VisuallyHidden) and add DialogDescription for screen-reader users.

When usage exceeds free max (e.g. demo user has 3 study plans vs max 1), the bar correctly turns red and shows '3/1' — visual is fine, but consider adding a 'Limite superato' label or an inline upgrade link next to that bar for clarity.

action_items: Fix SupportChat floating button overlap with Emergent badge — move it up (e.g. className 'fixed bottom-24 right-6 ...') so the user can actually click it. This is the only blocking issue.
Add DialogTitle (or VisuallyHidden DialogTitle) + DialogDescription inside Onboarding's DialogContent to silence Radix a11y errors.
Optional: surface a 'Sblocca' inline CTA next to any over-limit bar in the limits card.
critical_code_review_comments: extras.py duplicates the daily-window query for uploads/chat in two places (check_upload_limit in server.py, /me/limits, and chat_with_material). Extract a small helper count_today(collection, user_id, extra_filter) to avoid drift if the date math changes.

Free study-plan gate counts ALL plans for a user (db.study_plans.count_documents({user_id})). It does NOT filter past/expired plans — a Free user who once had a plan whose exam_date is past is permanently locked out. Consider counting only plans where max(days[].date) >= today, or adding a DELETE path.
/api/me/limits exposes no rate-limiting — a malicious client could poll it. Low priority but worth a small cache header.
support_chat dumps full SUPPORT_KB system prompt every turn — fine, but if KB grows consider trimming.
Onboarding stores completion in localStorage only; if the user clears storage or signs in on another device they see the tutorial again. Acceptable for MVP.
Logo.jsx LogoFull/LogoMark use /logo.png — confirmed served, but no <link rel='preload'> in index.html so first paint of header may flash. Minor.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (10/11 checks; SupportChat send blocked by overlay)

test_credentials: demo@studyos.app / demo1234 (free user with 3 existing plans, 2 materials)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data this iteration. Reused previous TEST_Chat_Material and TEST_Fotosintesi materials.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new backend endpoints (/me/limits, /support/chat, /support/chat/history, study-plan Free gate) are working. The 9-test pytest in /app/backend/tests/test_studyos_v3.py covers them; combine with test_studyos_extras.py (PayPal, push, notifications, material chat) and test_studyos.py for full regression. The single open frontend bug is SupportChat button being occluded by the Emergent badge — once main agent moves it (e.g. bottom-24 right-6), re-run the SupportChat send test to confirm the drawer opens, quick-chips render, and message send produces both user and assistant message bubbles. PayPal is LIVE — DO NOT click 'Completa' in the popup. Onboarding shows on first dashboard visit when localStorage 'studyos_onboarding_done' is unset; clear it to re-trigger.
rca of the issue: SupportChat overlap RCA: SupportChat.jsx places the floating action button with classNames 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 ... z-30'. The injected 'Made with Emergent' badge (<a id='emergent-badge'>) is also fixed at the bottom-right corner with a higher effective stacking position, so it intercepts pointer events on the support button. Mitigation: change SupportChat button to 'fixed bottom-24 right-6' (or 'left-6') so the two elements no longer overlap. z-index alone won't help because the badge already sits visually on top; physical separation is required.
